serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of roasted turkey breast without skin is approximately 85g (3 oz), equivalent to a palm-sized portion of cooked white meat.

nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database entry for roasted turkey breast without skin (cr_id F11050005). HPB values used as primary; USDA SR Legacy used for secondary nutrients not reported by HPB (saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, vitamins, minerals). gi_source.title: N/A — Roasted turkey breast without skin contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0g/100g); glycemic index is not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for this food as it contains 0.0g carbohydrate per 100g. Pure protein foods with negligible carbohydrate content do not produce a measurable glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. No GI value is assigned per Project Veritas v2 protocol for negligible-carbohydrate foods. glycemic_index field set to numeric 0 to satisfy template numeric requirement; gi_category is not_applicable to reflect the true clinical status.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (99mg/100g) and favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (249mg K : 99mg Na)
hypertension.guidance: Roasted turkey breast without skin is a hypertension-friendly lean protein with only 99mg sodium and 249mg potassium per 100g; individuals with hypertension should avoid brined, pre-seasoned, or deli-sliced turkey products that may contain >500mg sodium per 100g, and should not add high-sodium gravies or sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate (0.0g/100g) and zero sugar (0.0g/100g) with very high protein (30.13g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Roasted turkey breast without skin has zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, producing no glycaemic impact; its very high protein content (30.13g/100g) supports satiety and slows gastric emptying, making it an excellent protein choice for individuals managing type 2 diabetes when paired with non-starchy vegetables and a balanced meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (2.08g/100g) and low saturated fat (0.62g/100g) with high lean protein
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Roasted turkey breast without skin is a cardioprotective lean protein with only 2.08g total fat and 0.62g saturated fat per 100g and 80mg cholesterol; it supports heart-healthy dietary patterns and can be consumed regularly by individuals with cardiovascular disease as part of a diet low in saturated fat and sodium.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of positive anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, antioxidants, polyphenols); lean protein supports muscle preservation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Roasted turkey breast without skin is a non-inflammatory lean protein with very low saturated fat (0.62g/100g) that supports muscle preservation important in rheumatoid arthritis, but it lacks positive anti-inflammatory factors such as omega-3 fatty acids, antioxidants, or polyphenols; it should be paired with anti-inflammatory foods such as oily fish, turmeric-seasoned vegetables, or cruciferous vegetables to enhance dietary benefit for RA management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted turkey breast without skin is cooked white meat from the breast portion of a turkey, prepared by dry-heat roasting without the skin, yielding a tender, slightly firm, and mildly flavoured lean protein. Per 100g it provides 147 kcal, 30.13g protein, 2.08g total fat (0.62g saturated), 0.0g carbohydrate, 0.0g sugar, 99mg sodium, 249mg potassium, 9mg calcium, 0.71mg iron, and 80mg cholesterol. The absence of carbohydrate and sugar means it produces no glycaemic response, making it an ideal protein source for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Its very high protein density supports satiety, muscle preservation, and metabolic health. The low saturated fat content and favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio make it a heart-healthy and blood-pressure-friendly food. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. Turkey breast is widely consumed in Western and American culinary traditions, particularly during festive occasions such as Thanksgiving and Christmas, but is also a staple lean protein in everyday meal preparation. It is commonly served sliced alongside roasted vegetables, salads, or whole-grain sides. Individuals should avoid processed or deli-sliced turkey products, which may contain added sodium, sugars, and preservatives not present in freshly roasted skinless turkey breast.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id F11050005, confidence 0.95). Secondary nutrients (saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, B-vitamins, minerals) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 05165 (turkey breast, meat only, roasted), which is an exact match for this food. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database and USDA SR Legacy for roasted turkey breast without skin — this is naturally zero as turkey breast is a pure animal protein food containing no carbohydrate by composition. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal-derived food with no plant fibre. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible/trace per USDA SR Legacy). Vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for cooked turkey breast). Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo; USDA SR Legacy reports trace retinol activity equivalent for turkey breast but value varies by preparation; excluded to avoid unsupported precision. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo; USDA SR Legacy reports minimal vitamin D in turkey breast but data is limited for roasted preparation. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo or with sufficient precision in USDA SR Legacy for this preparation. Glycemic index field is set to numeric 0 to satisfy template numeric type requirement; gi_category is set to not_applicable to reflect the true clinical status that GI is not measurable for a food containing 0.0g carbohydrate per 100g. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0. Halal status noted as true for plain roasted turkey breast without additives; kosher status depends on slaughter method and is not confirmed. Ontology codes: FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03311349 is a defensible match for turkey breast food; FoodEx2 code A0FKP is used as the closest defensible match for roasted turkey meat without skin; SNOMED CT 226955001 is a defensible match for turkey breast as food. No multilingual local-language names are required as this is a Western/American generic food item, not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish.
